message:
chore: sync concurrent agent changes

Sync changes from concurrent agents.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Content ideas:
- Top row: overall platform status, current env, uptime, active incidents.
- Middle: service health cards + deployment status.

 {"id":"bd-lxky","title":"Unit tests: animation subsystem determinism + reduce-motion","description":"Add deterministic unit tests for the demo_showcase animation subsystem.

Why:
- The showcase will lean heavily on motion; we need confidence it won’t become flaky or timing-dependent.
- CI must be able to validate animation code paths without real sleeps.

Test coverage:
- Given a fixed dt (e.g., 16ms) and fixed initial/target values, stepping the animation yields deterministic values.
- Animations stay bounded (no NaNs, no runaway values, no panics on extreme dt).
- Reduce-motion / `--no-animations` behavior:
  - animated values immediately snap to target (or the subsystem becomes a no-op) according to the design.
  - no tick scheduling when animations are disabled.

Acceptance:
- Tests do not depend on wall-clock time.
- Failures clearly report which animation invariant broke.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.665155320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:21:52.514623411Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:21:52.514494150Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["anim","demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.688967780Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.679805544Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-3q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.684774144Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-mwty","title":"Logs: implement live log viewer (viewport + follow mode)","description":"Implement the Logs page core UI (the thing users will stare at for minutes).

Why:
- Logs are the heartbeat of an ops console.
- This is where styling + performance + interaction quality are most obvious.

Requirements:
- Viewport that can scroll back through a large buffer.
- Follow mode (tail -f) toggle:
  - when enabled, viewport stays pinned to latest entries
  - when user scrolls up, follow pauses automatically
  - a clear UI hint indicates "paused" vs "following"
- Selection (optional but great):
  - highlight current line for copy/export

Performance:
- Must handle thousands of lines without flicker.
- Avoid rebuilding huge strings unnecessarily; prefer rendering only the visible window.

Integration:
- Uses structured log entries + formatter (`Logs: styled log formatting using charmed_log`).
- Filtering/search is layered on later but the core viewer should support it cleanly.

Acceptance:
- Smooth scrolling and no flicker.
- Follow mode behavior matches user expectations.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:36.085412578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:53:36.090025763Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:53:36.089960261Z","close_reason":"Implemented LogsPage with live log viewer: viewport scrolling, follow mode (tail -f), auto-pause on scroll up, status bar with level counts and position. Added 8 tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logs","viewport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mwty","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:33.789901378Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-mwty","depends_on_id":"bd-2qx1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:36.112421707Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-mwty","depends_on_id":"bd-32pp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:36.416170919Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ndn1","title":"EPIC: Property-Based Testing (proptest)","description":"Property-based testing using proptest for algorithmic and numeric code.

## Philosophy
- Let the computer find edge cases humans miss
- Test invariants, not specific inputs
- Shrink failing cases to minimal reproductions
- Focus on numeric/algorithmic code

## Target Areas
1. harmonica - spring physics calculations
   - Animation value convergence
   - Velocity damping behavior
   - Frame timing consistency

2. lipgloss - dimension calculations
   - Width/height calculations with padding/margin
   - Position calculations
   - Unicode width handling

3. bubbles - component state machines
   - Viewport scroll bounds
   - Progress bar percentages
   - Spinner frame cycling

4. glamour - text wrapping
   - Word wrap boundaries
   - Unicode character handling
   - ANSI sequence preservation

## Property Examples
- For any input, width >= content width
- Animation always converges to target
- Scroll position always in valid range
- Progress percentage always 0-100","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:06.367379778Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:59:12.009040062Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ndn1","depends_on_id":"bd-665u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:06.381903246Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":25,"issue_id":"bd-ndn1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"PROPERTY TESTING STRATEGY:

Property tests complement unit tests by finding edge cases that humans miss.
They're especially valuable for:
- Numeric/mathematical code (harmonica springs)
- Dimension calculations (lipgloss widths)
- State machines (bubbles components)
- Text processing (glamour wrapping)

PROPTEST PATTERNS:

1. INVARIANT PROPERTIES - 'This must ALWAYS be true'
   ```rust
   proptest! {
       #[test]
       fn progress_always_valid(pct in any::<f64>()) {
           let p = Progress::new().set_percent(pct);
           assert!(p.percent() >= 0.0 && p.percent() <= 1.0);
       }
   }
   ```

2. ROUND-TRIP PROPERTIES - 'Encode then decode equals original'
   ```rust
   proptest! {
       #[test]
       fn style_render_preserves_content(s in "[a-z ]{0,50}") {
           let styled = Style::new().render(&s);
           let stripped = strip_ansi(&styled);
           assert_eq!(stripped.trim(), s.trim());
       }
   }
   ```

3. COMPARISON PROPERTIES - 'This equals that'
   ```rust
   proptest! {
       #[test]
       fn batch_order_independent(cmds in vec(any_cmd(), 0..10)) {
           // Results should be same regardless of batch order
       }
   }
   ```

SHRINKING: Let proptest shrink failures to minimal reproducible cases.
Always save the seed for reproducibility in CI.","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:59:12Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-nw3u","title":"Fix run_conformance JSON timestamp (use real RFC3339)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-21T21:44:06.445942217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:58:47.358436941Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T21:58:47.358359595Z","close_reason":"Fixed: chrono_lite_timestamp() now uses chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339_opts() for real RFC3339 timestamps","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
